title: erythroxylum tenue
intro: Erythroxylum tenue, also known as the "Coca of the Andes," is a plant species native to the Andean region of South America, belonging to the Erythroxylum genus. The leaves of this plant have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, as well as their ability to increase alertness and reduce fatigue. The health benefits of Erythroxylum tenue include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve cognitive function, and aid in the management of chronic pain. In herbal preparations, the leaves are typically dried, then used in tea or tincture form, although the plant's psychoactive properties have also been studied for potential use in pharmaceutical applications.
